_about: Vulture AI (OVRLKD Studio) configuration. Copy this file to 'config.json' (same folder, next to studio.py) and edit the values for YOUR machine. Nothing here is machine-specific by design: pick your own drive letter, username and install location.
_how_it_works: The loader (vulture/config.py) layers three sources: built-in defaults  <-  AUTO-DETECTION (scans all drives for ComfyUI / Ollama / Python)  <-  this file. So you only need to set the values auto-detection cannot guess (or the ones it guessed wrong). Any key you delete or leave as "" (empty string) falls back to auto-detect, then to a sensible default.
_notes: Forward slashes '/' and back slashes '\\' both work; the loader normalises them for Windows.
Keys starting with '_' or '//' are comments and are ignored by the loader.
Leave a path as "" to let auto-detection fill it in.
This is drive-agnostic: if your ComfyUI lives on E: or C:, just change the drive letter.

paths._comment: Absolute locations of the tools. Change the drive letter/username to match your PC.
paths._comfy_dir: The ComfyUI application folder (the one that contains main.py and the 'models' + 'custom_nodes' subfolders).
paths._comfy_python: python.exe of ComfyUI's virtual environment. For the portable build this is '<portable>/python_embeded/python.exe'. Leave "" to auto-detect.
paths._comfy_models_dir: Where checkpoints/unet/vae/... live. Empty => derived as <comfy_dir>/models.
paths._comfy_input_dir: ComfyUI input folder (source images are copied here). Empty => derived as <comfy_dir>/input.
paths._output_dir: Where generated images/videos are written and opened from.
paths._tools_dir: Optional: the 'ai-memory-tools' folder holding start-webui.cmd / start-local-memory.cmd / start-tunnel.cmd (Chat + Code-RAG + VPS tunnel). Leave "" if you do not use these extras.
paths._launchers_dir: Folder that holds OVRLKD-KI.cmd, KI-Coder.cmd, KI-Status.cmd. Empty => the repo root (folder that contains studio.py).
paths._tripo: Optional Image->3D (TripoSR). Leave the whole block as "" if you don't use it; the 3D button will offer to run the setup script.
paths._ollama_exe: Path to ollama.exe. Empty => auto-detect (%LOCALAPPDATA%/Programs/Ollama/ollama.exe or PATH).
paths._ollama_models_dir: Where Ollama stores models (sets the OLLAMA_MODELS env var). Empty => Ollama default (~/.ollama/models).
paths._system_python: System Python 3.11 (used by KI-Coder.cmd helpers). Empty => auto-detect.
paths._aider_python: python.exe of the Aider coding-agent venv. Optional (coding agent only).
paths._caches: Optional: keep HuggingFace + pip caches off C:. Empty => system defaults.

network._comment: Host + ports. Only change if a port clashes with something else.

runtime._vram_tier: auto | s | m | l | xl. 'auto' asks nvidia-smi. s=6GB, m=12GB, l=16GB, xl=24GB+. Controls the ComfyUI VRAM flag and the coding-agent context size.
runtime._comfy_vram_flag: Override the ComfyUI memory flag. Empty => chosen from vram_tier ('--lowvram' for s, '--fast' for m/l/xl). Set e.g. '--normalvram' or '--novram' to force.
runtime._comfy_extra_args: Extra CLI args appended to the ComfyUI start command (e.g. '--use-pytorch-cross-attention').
runtime._enhance_model: Ollama model used by the 'optimise prompt' feature (turns casual/German text into an English FLUX prompt).
runtime._coder_model: Default Ollama model for the coding agent. The author prefers 'qwen3.5:9b' on bigger GPUs; 'qwen2.5-coder:7b' is the safe 6GB default.

models._comment: Filenames used inside the ComfyUI workflows. These must match what the installer put in <comfy_models_dir>. Change only if you downloaded a different quant/variant.
